decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Iron-Shield Elf. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board and protect the combo pieces. The mana base is carefully crafted to ensure smooth land drops and consistency in casting the key cards. Overall, the deck aims to assemble the combo quickly and efficiently to secure victory.

The main concept of the deck is to aggressively attack the opponent using low-cost spells and creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Cori-Steel Cutter, and Soul-Scar Mage. The deck focuses on dealing damage quickly and efficiently through cards like Burst Lightning and Stormchaser's Talent. The mana base is optimized to support the red and blue spells in the deck, with cards like Steam Vents and Spirebluff Canal. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast-paced, aggressive strategy that applies pressure from the beginning of the game.

The main focus of this Golgari Aggro deck is to maintain constant pressure on the opponent by efficiently using low-cost creatures and removal spells. By disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and deploying aggressive creatures like Ouroboroid and Badgermole Cub, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their game plan. The deck also utilizes land destruction with cards like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Boseiju, Who Endures to disrupt the opponent's mana base and gain an advantage in the mid to late game.

This Izzet Control deck focuses on controlling the board through various spells and abilities. It aims to stall opponents with disruptive cards like Divide by Zero and Combustion Technique, while also drawing cards and gaining card advantage with spells like Accumulate Wisdom and Consider. The deck's win conditions include overwhelming opponents with powerful creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter and Gran-Gran, as well as utilizing burn spells like Torch the Tower to finish off opponents. With a mix of control and aggression, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly getting Arclight Phoenix into the graveyard and then using spells like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse to bring it back into play. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Opt and Consider to help dig for key pieces. The inclusion of Picklock Prankster allows for additional ways to bring back Arclight Phoenix while also providing extra damage to the opponent. Treasure Cruise provides additional card draw, while the deck's mana base allows for flexibility in casting spells and activating abilities.

The main concept of this Izzet Control deck is to maintain control of the game through card advantage, removal spells, and counter spells. The deck focuses on manipulating the opponent's resources while building up its own to eventually overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and creatures. By strategically using cards like Divide by Zero, Gran-Gran, and Stormchaser's Talent, the player can control the tempo of the game and dictate the flow of combat. Additionally, the deck includes some direct damage spells like Combustion Technique and Torch the Tower to finish off opponents quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly develop a board presence with efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage. The deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain tempo throughout the game by utilizing cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Cori-Steel Cutter. By combining aggression with disruption, this UR Aggro deck seeks to overwhelm opponents and secure victory before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on building up a strong board presence quickly and overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and combat tricks. Using cards like Defiant Strike and Slip Through Space to buff your creatures and draw cards, along with powerful aggro creatures like Favored Hoplite and Elusive Otter, this deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and often. Cards like Gods Willing and Sheltered by Ghosts help protect your creatures from removal spells, allowing you to maintain your aggressive strategy. With a mix of creatures, spells, and land cards, this deck is well-rounded and versatile in its gameplay.

This Red Deck Wins aggro deck focuses on fast-paced aggression and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. With a mix of efficient creatures like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger, along with powerful burn spells such as Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, this deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional sources of damage, while cards like Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage help to push through damage and deal with opposing threats. The deck's aggressive game plan is further supported by cards like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Screaming Nemesis, ensuring that the pressure remains high throughout the game.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful engine that can overwhelm opponents. The deck includes cards to search for combo pieces, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and protect the combo once assembled. With a mix of creature threats, removal spells, and card advantage engines, the deck aims to control the game until it can assemble its key pieces and dominate the battlefield with high-powered creatures and powerful abilities.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using a variety of spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It includes a mix of hand disruption with Thoughtseize, removal spells like Fatal Push, along with bounce effects such as Boomerang Basics and Nowhere to Run. The deck also includes a selection of lands that provide flexibility and utility, like Geier Reach Sanitarium and Field of Ruin. With the ability to generate card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver its opponents.

The main concept of the deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's game plan and maintain board control. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds a tribal element to the deck, providing additional value and synergy with creature spells. The deck also focuses on mana fixing through cards like Floodfarm Verge and Fountainport to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana for powerful spells like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict.

The deck revolves around utilizing Lotus Field and various land cards to generate a large amount of mana quickly, enabling powerful combo plays. By sacrificing lands with Lotus Field's ability and using cards like Scapeshift and Formidable Speaker, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with big plays and finish the game with cards like Thassa's Oracle or Culling Ritual. The deck also includes cards like Arboreal Grazer and The Wandering Minstrel to ramp up mana production and ensure a smooth combo execution.

This deck focuses on generating a powerful board presence by combining the abilities of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. Utilizing cards like Bitter Triumph, Concealed Courtyard, and Cache Grab to manage resources and disrupt opponents, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with creatures like Formidable Speaker, Overlord of the Balemurk, and Skysovereign, Consul Flagship. Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command help to control the battlefield while cards like Lush Portico and Voice of Victory provide additional support. By efficiently utilizing its resources and combo potential, this deck aims to secure victory through its powerful synergies.

This deck focuses on combining Greasefang with Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy. It aims to control the board with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push while building up resources with Monument to Endurance and Mycosynth Gardens. The various lands in the deck help to support the overall strategy, allowing for flexibility in mana production and protection. The inclusion of Bleachbone Verge and Brightclimb Pathway helps provide additional value and options for the player. Ultimately, the goal is to assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a strong finish.

This aggressive deck focuses on quickly casting spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard to the battlefield, while also using cheap removal and card draw to control the board and outpace opponents. The deck utilizes a variety of cheap spells, cantrips, and mana sources to consistently bring out Arclight Phoenix and overwhelm opponents with quick, efficient plays. The inclusion of disruptive creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter and Picklock Prankster adds additional pressure and disruption to keep opponents on their toes. The deck aims to be fast, versatile, and relentless in its attacks.
